---
title: "Introduction"
subtitle: "Pastafarianism and the question of religion"
date: "2026-10-06"
status: "published"
author: "Prof. Jane Smith"
organization: "My University"
institute: "Institute of Something"
email: "jane.smith@university.edu"
course: "My Course"
term: "WS 2026"
lang: "en"
format:
  html:
    argdown:
      mode: web-component
      sourceHighlighter: web-component
      config: ../../argdown.config.json
filters:
  - ../../node_modules/.bin/argdown-filter
bibliography: ../../bibliography.bib
---

## {{< fa circle-info >}} Overview

In 2005, Bobby Henderson — a physics graduate from Oregon State University — sent an open letter to the Kansas State Board of Education. The Board had just voted to allow *intelligent design* alongside evolutionary theory in public school science classes. Henderson proposed that if one supernatural creation story got class time, so should another: the **Flying Spaghetti Monster (FSM)**, a deity made of spaghetti and meatballs who created the universe "while quite drunk."

The letter went viral. Henderson's movement — **Pastafarianism** (a portmanteau of *pasta* and *Rastafarianism*) — grew into the **Church of the Flying Spaghetti Monster**. Its followers wear colanders as ritual headgear, revere pirates as holy figures, and attribute global warming to the decline of piracy.

For philosophers and legal scholars, Pastafarianism raises a serious question: if a belief system fulfils all standard criteria for religion — cosmology, scripture, ritual, community — does the *satirical intent of its founder* change anything?

### Henderson's Founding Argument

```argdown
[Equal time]: FSM creationism deserves equal time in public school science class.

<Equality argument>

(1) If one supernatural creation theory receives time in public school science classes,
    all others deserve equal time.
(2) Intelligent design received time in Kansas public school science classes.
----
(3) [Equal time]
```

**Key concepts for this session:** satirical religion, intelligent design, church–state separation, argument reconstruction.



 [Slides in full screen](../slides/intro-slides.html){target="_blank"}   | [Slides as PDF](../slides/intro-slides.pdf){target="_blank"} 


```{=html}
<iframe class="slide-deck" src="../slides/intro-slides.html" height="420" width="747" style="border: 1px solid #123233;"></iframe>
```



 




## {{< fa folder-open >}} Materials







- **Slides**: [Introduction](../slides/intro-slides.html) | [{{< fa file-pdf >}}](../slides/intro-slides.pdf)








- **Handout**: [Introduction](../handouts/intro-handout.html) | [{{< fa file-pdf >}}](../handouts/intro-handout.pdf)








- **Notes**: Introduction | [{{< fa file-pdf >}}](../notes/intro-notes.pdf)
